read.title: Capital Steals Attention And Calls It Freedom
read.subtitle: A cleaned reading of Secret History #25
read.voice_policy: Direct argumentative prose derived from the lecture. Avoid repeated third-person framing. Do not impersonate first-person authorship except when quoting verified source material.
read.trace_policy: Paragraph-level transcript refs. Controversial historical claims remain attributed to this dated source.
read.dek: Capital is not a thing you own. It is a system that captures attention, manufactures anxiety, destroys stored security, and then moves somewhere else. Secret societies enter as the hidden trust machinery of capital that refuses place, nation, and responsibility.
read.thesis: The lecture is Jiang's theory of capital as spiritual extraction. Economics begins as a fight over grain, but grain is too concrete and peasants can stop once they have enough. Capital improves by becoming abstract: freedom replaces slavery, equity makes people work harder, money becomes God, debt creates anxiety, and wars or depressions burn down the granary. Once people become too exploited, the landlord class moves. That mobile landlord is transnational capital, and its practical form is the secret society: a ritual structure that lets elites trust one another, displace agency onto Satan or Saturn, train dependent initiates, pool money through banks, and pass the same merchant-oligarchy system from Venice to the Dutch Republic, England, and America.

opening.heading: Core Reading
opening.text: The first reversal is brutal because it sounds like economics and then stops being economics. Capital is not money, wealth, grain, gold, or a corporation. Capital is a mechanism for extracting human energy. Since consciousness is the universe, energy means attention and focus. A landlord does not merely want rent from the peasant; he wants the peasant's whole nervous system pointed at work. The history of capital is therefore the history of better attention capture: give people the illusion of freedom, make money so abstract that it becomes God, create debt and poverty so anxiety never stops, then periodically destroy stored wealth so nobody can rest. If the peasants finally revolt, capital moves. The ruling class has no homeland. It has only capital.

beats.heading: Capital Is Attention Extraction
beats.summary: The lecture begins by replacing the economics definition of capital with a consciousness definition: capital extracts focus, and modern society gets better at making people focus through freedom, abstraction, anxiety, and destruction.

paragraphs.text: The peasant with grain is not yet maximally useful. Grain feeds a family, can be stored, and eventually lets a person stop. The landlord wants something more total: not merely rent, but concentration. The first improvement is freedom itself. Direct slavery is clumsy. Give the worker control, choice, a vote, or equity, and the worker begins to police his own attention.

beats.heading: Capital Learns To Move
beats.summary: When anxiety becomes revolt, capital escapes place. Jiang calls this transnational capital and identifies secret societies as its trust system and agency-displacement ritual.

paragraphs.text: Transnational capital is the landlord after he learns to leave. He goes to Dubai, Hong Kong, Singapore, anywhere taxes are lower, conflict is avoidable, and investment is better. He does not betray his country because country was never the object of loyalty. Capital was.

beats.heading: Merchant Oligarchy Gets More Abstract
beats.summary: Venice, the Dutch Republic, England, and America are treated as four versions of the same private-interest system, improving through banking, trade control, diplomacy, Protestantism, and the Enlightenment.

paragraphs.text: The historical sequence is Venice, the Dutch Republic, England, America. Each calls itself something respectable, often republic or democracy, but Jiang reads each as a merchant oligarchy: private interests coordinating through secret society rather than one king. Its tools are banking, navy, and diplomacy. Banking is the key because it extracts energy across time and space.

paragraphs.text: Then the Enlightenment removes active God and leaves a vacant throne. Money takes it. The Dutch move capital to England through William of Orange and the Bank of England, a private bank guaranteed by Parliament. The private banker keeps profit; the people guarantee loss. The same structure later appears as the Federal Reserve. Venice supplies the prototype: a poor, hard-to-invade trading city that becomes a parasite on empires.

beats.heading: America Inherits The Machine

India is turned into a resource colony: manufacturing destroyed, agriculture imposed, British goods purchased, local elites given a way to store wealth safely in England. The flag can leave and the system can remain, because offshore banking aligns local elites with the empire against their own people.

read.source_note: This read preserves Jiang's dated December 9, 2025 lecture argument, including controversial claims about secret societies, Satan or Saturn rituals, trauma programming, Jesuits, Freemasons, British finance, American monopolists, technology firms, U.S. currency, and World War III. It presents those claims as the source's internal model, not as independent historical, psychological, or political verification. Several spans contain ASR noise: 'secret societies' is sometimes rendered as 'six societies,' hashish as 'hashes,' Loyola as 'L Oreal,' Venice's marshes as 'Marcians,' and the next class topic as an uncertain phrase. The read follows the clear argumentative movement without reproducing those damaged forms.
